title: Waves & Sound
subject: physics
summary: Waves and sound are common physics topics because they connect formulas, graphs, and real-life situations.
SPTG usually tests wave speed, frequency, wavelength, period, sound propagation, and echo problems.
Harder questions may include medium effects, wave type comparison, and graph interpretation.
coreConcepts: A wave transfers energy without transferring matter overall.
Wave speed, frequency, and wavelength are related by v = fλ.
Frequency is the number of oscillations per second.
Period is the time for one complete oscillation.
Transverse waves oscillate perpendicular to the direction of travel.
Longitudinal waves oscillate parallel to the direction of travel.
Sound is a longitudinal mechanical wave and needs a medium.
Sound travels faster in solids than liquids, and faster in liquids than gases.
When a wave enters a new medium, its frequency stays the same while speed and wavelength may change.
derive: If one wave cycle travels one wavelength λ in one period T, then speed = distance / time = λ / T.
Since frequency f = 1/T, substituting gives v = fλ.
If f increases while v stays constant, then λ must decrease.
For an echo, sound travels to the surface and back, so total distance is 2d.
Thus echo time t satisfies 2d = vt, so d = vt/2.
formulas: Total distance in echo problem: 2d = vt
traps: Do not forget that echo involves a round trip.
Frequency stays constant when a wave changes medium; wavelength changes instead.
A larger amplitude does not mean a larger wave speed.
Period and frequency are reciprocals, not independent quantities.
Do not confuse loudness with pitch: pitch depends on frequency.

sptgSet.question: A wave has frequency 5 Hz and wavelength 2 m. Find its speed.
A wave has period 0.2 s. Find its frequency.
A sound wave travels at 340 m/s and has frequency 170 Hz. Find wavelength.
A wave has frequency 50 Hz and speed 100 m/s. Find wavelength.
A wave has wavelength 4 m and speed 20 m/s. Find frequency.
A sound echo is heard 2 s after a shout. If sound speed is 340 m/s, find the distance to the wall.
Classify sound waves as transverse or longitudinal.
A wave enters a new medium and its speed decreases. What happens to wavelength if frequency stays constant?
A wave has frequency 200 Hz. Find its period.

hardSet.question: A wave speed is 300 m/s and frequency is increased from 50 Hz to 75 Hz in the same medium. Find the new wavelength.
A wave has wavelength 0.5 m and period 0.01 s. Find speed.
A submarine sends a sound pulse and receives the echo after 4 s. If sound speed in water is 1500 m/s, find the depth.
A wave takes 0.5 s to complete one cycle. How many cycles occur in 10 s?
A wave travels 120 m in 8 s. If its frequency is 3 Hz, find wavelength.
If frequency doubles in the same medium, what happens to wavelength?
Which travels faster: sound in air, water, or steel?
A sound source has frequency 500 Hz. Is this pitch higher or lower than a 250 Hz source?
A wave enters another medium. Its speed changes from 12 m/s to 18 m/s while frequency remains 6 Hz. Find the new wavelength.
A wave graph shows 8 complete cycles in 4 s. Find frequency and period.

pat3Set.question: A string wave has speed 40 m/s and wavelength 0.8 m. Find frequency and period.
A sound pulse travels to a cliff and back in 1.2 s. If speed of sound is 340 m/s, find the distance to the cliff.
A wave passes from medium A to medium B. Speed changes from 24 m/s to 16 m/s, wavelength in A is 3 m. Find wavelength in B.
A wave has equation data implying frequency 25 Hz and speed 50 m/s. Find wavelength.
In 0.2 s, a source produces 40 oscillations. Find frequency and period.
A wave travels 300 m in 15 s. If wavelength is 10 m, find frequency.
A sonar pulse is sent downward in water of speed 1500 m/s. If the echo returns in 0.8 s, find depth.
A transverse wave has amplitude 3 cm and wavelength 20 cm. Which quantity affects energy transfer more directly here: amplitude or wavelength?
A wave source frequency is unchanged but wavelength doubles after entering a new medium. What happens to wave speed?
A sound wave cannot travel through space between planets. State why.
pat3Set.answer: Because sound needs a material medium and space is nearly vacuum
